structured.summary: The paper presents UniDoc, a unified pretraining framework for document understanding that integrates multimodal inputs (textual and visual) using a hierarchical transformer with gated cross-attention. UniDoc employs three self-supervised pretraining tasks to learn joint representations and demonstrates improved performance on multiple downstream document understanding tasks.

strengths.point: Unified multimodal pretraining framework that jointly learns visual and textual features end-to-end.
strengths.evidence: Section 3.1 describes the architecture combining visual feature extraction, sentence encoding, and gated cross-attention transformer; Section 4.3 shows improved downstream task performance.

strengths.point: Introduction of novel pretraining tasks: Masked Sentence Modeling, Visual Contrastive Learning, and Vision-Language Alignment.
strengths.evidence: Section 3.2 details these three complementary self-supervised objectives designed to learn contextual and aligned multimodal representations.

strengths.point: Extensive empirical evaluation on diverse downstream tasks including form understanding, receipt understanding, document classification, and document object detection.
strengths.evidence: Section 4.2 and 4.3 present results on FUNSD, CORD, RVL-CDIP, and PubLayNet datasets, demonstrating consistent improvements over baselines and prior state-of-the-art.

strengths.point: Hierarchical modeling of documents at the sentence and region level to overcome input length limitations and capture document structure.
strengths.evidence: Section 1 and 3.1 discuss the hierarchical transformer encoder that models words forming sentences and sentences forming documents, enabling efficient and semantically meaningful input representation.

weaknesses.point: The sentence encoder is frozen during pretraining and fine-tuning, potentially limiting full end-to-end learning.
weaknesses.evidence: Section 4.1 and 5 mention freezing the pretrained sentence encoder due to computational constraints.

weaknesses.point: The pretraining corpus is limited to 1M images sampled from IIT-CDIP, smaller than datasets used by some competing methods.
weaknesses.evidence: Section 4.1 notes using 1M images for pretraining, while LayoutLM and LayoutLMv2 use 11M images.

weaknesses.point: No error bars or multiple runs reported, limiting assessment of result robustness.
weaknesses.evidence: Checklist section indicates no error bars reported; no mention of multiple random seeds or runs.

weaknesses.point: The model relies on OCR quality, and EasyOCR is used which is weaker than commercial OCR engines, potentially limiting performance on some tasks.
weaknesses.evidence: Section 4.3 discusses OCR impact on document classification and compares EasyOCR with Tesseract.

weaknesses.point: The paper does not provide code or data release, hindering reproducibility.
weaknesses.evidence: Checklist section indicates code and data are not provided.

weaknesses.point: Limited discussion on handling multi-page or multilingual documents.
weaknesses.evidence: Section 5 mentions these as future directions but no experiments or methods addressing them.

weaknesses.point: The gating mechanism for cross-attention is described but lacks ablation or detailed analysis of its contribution.
weaknesses.evidence: Section 3.1 describes gated cross-attention but no ablation study isolating its effect is presented.

weaknesses.point: The impact of the visual backbone architecture choice beyond ResNet-50 is not explored.
weaknesses.evidence: Section 4.3 briefly mentions the backbone but experiments only use ResNet-50.

questions.question: Have you considered jointly fine-tuning the sentence encoder along with the visual backbone and cross-attention encoder to potentially improve performance?
questions.motivation: Joint end-to-end training could enable better multimodal feature alignment and representation learning.

questions.question: Can you provide more detailed ablation studies on the gating mechanism in the cross-attention module to quantify its contribution?
questions.motivation: Understanding the gating mechanism's impact would clarify its importance and guide future improvements.

questions.question: How does UniDoc handle documents with multiple pages or multilingual content, and what are the challenges in extending to these scenarios?
questions.motivation: Many real-world documents are multipage and multilingual; addressing these would increase practical applicability.

questions.question: Do you have plans to release the code and pretrained models to facilitate reproducibility and further research?
questions.motivation: Open sourcing would benefit the community and validate the reported results.

critique_points.point_001: The sentence encoder is frozen during pretraining and fine-tuning, potentially limiting full end-to-end learning. (Section 4.1 and 5 mention freezing the pretrained sentence encoder due to computational constraints.)
critique_points.point_002: The pretraining corpus is limited to 1M images sampled from IIT-CDIP, smaller than datasets used by some competing methods. (Section 4.1 notes using 1M images for pretraining, while LayoutLM and LayoutLMv2 use 11M images.)
critique_points.point_003: No error bars or multiple runs reported, limiting assessment of result robustness. (Checklist section indicates no error bars reported; no mention of multiple random seeds or runs.)
critique_points.point_004: The model relies on OCR quality, and EasyOCR is used which is weaker than commercial OCR engines, potentially limiting performance on some tasks. (Section 4.3 discusses OCR impact on document classification and compares EasyOCR with Tesseract.)
critique_points.point_005: The paper does not provide code or data release, hindering reproducibility. (Checklist section indicates code and data are not provided.)
critique_points.point_006: Limited discussion on handling multi-page or multilingual documents. (Section 5 mentions these as future directions but no experiments or methods addressing them.)
critique_points.point_007: The gating mechanism for cross-attention is described but lacks ablation or detailed analysis of its contribution. (Section 3.1 describes gated cross-attention but no ablation study isolating its effect is presented.)
critique_points.point_008: The impact of the visual backbone architecture choice beyond ResNet-50 is not explored. (Section 4.3 briefly mentions the backbone but experiments only use ResNet-50.)
critique_points.point_009: Have you considered jointly fine-tuning the sentence encoder along with the visual backbone and cross-attention encoder to potentially improve performance? (Joint end-to-end training could enable better multimodal feature alignment and representation learning.)
critique_points.point_010: Can you provide more detailed ablation studies on the gating mechanism in the cross-attention module to quantify its contribution? (Understanding the gating mechanism's impact would clarify its importance and guide future improvements.)
critique_points.point_011: How does UniDoc handle documents with multiple pages or multilingual content, and what are the challenges in extending to these scenarios? (Many real-world documents are multipage and multilingual; addressing these would increase practical applicability.)
critique_points.point_012: Do you have plans to release the code and pretrained models to facilitate reproducibility and further research? (Open sourcing would benefit the community and validate the reported results.)
